Knapp, S. 2024. A revision of Lycianthes (Solanaceae) in tropical Asia. PhytoKeys 245: 1-106.

Solanaceae Source: A global taxonomic resource for the nightshade family

Naturwiss. Vereins Bremen 24 (1920) 511; ≡ Solanum lagunense Elmer , LPB 1 (1908) 341 ; Merrill, EPFP 3 (1923) 426; Type: Elmer 9425 (K, lecto; E, K, LAE, isolecto), Luzon: Laguna, Los Baños, Apr-1906. = Lycianthes banahaensis ssp. manucaling (Elmer) Bitter , Lycianthes (1919) 510; ≡ Solanum manucaling Elmer , LPB 2 (1910) 732 ; Merrill, EPFP 3 (1923) 427; Type: Elmer 10489 (BISH, lecto; A, BM, CAL, E, G, GH, HBG, K, L, LE, MO, NY, US, W, isolecto), Mindanao: Davao del Sur, Todaya (Mt Apo), May-1909. = Lycianthes anisophylla Bitter , Abh. Naturwiss. Naturwiss. Vereins Bremen 24 (1920) 513; Type: Merrill 3046 (B, holo, lost; US, lecto; B, K, NY, isolecto), Masbate: Aug-1903. ≠ Solanum anisophyllum auct. non Van Heurck & Müll.Arg. ; Elmer, LPB 8 (1915) 2830.

ALABAT, BILIRAN, BOHOL, CATANDUANES, GUIMARAS, LEYTE, LUZON: Abra, Albay, Cagayan, Cavite, Ilocos Norte, Ilocos Sur, Kalinga, Laguna, Mountain Province, Nueva Vizcaya, Quezon, Sorsogon, MASBATE, MINDANAO: Agusan, Agusan del Norte, Bukidnon, Davao, Davao Oriental, Davao del Sur, Lanao del Norte, Lanao del Sur, South Cotabato, Surigao, Surigao del Norte, Zamboanga, Zamboanga del Sur, MINDORO, PANAY, POLILLO, SAMAR, SIARGAO, TABLAS.

Spec. Nov. Regni Veg. 18 (1922) 321; Type: Wenzel 500 (G, holo; BM, F, MO, iso), Leyte: 19-Sep-1919.

BALABAC, BASILAN, BILIRAN, BOHOL, CATANDUANES, LEYTE, LUZON: Apayao, Aurora, Camarines Norte (photos), Camarines Sur, Laguna, Quezon, Sorsogon, MINDANAO: Agusan del Norte, Agusan del Sur, Bukidnon, Davao del Sur, Maguindanao, Sarangani, Surigao, Zamboanga del Sur, MINDORO, PANAY, SAMAR, SIARGAO, TAWI-TAWI.
species.notes: In most islands and provinces, epiphytic in damp primary forests at low and medium elevation. 100-1000m.

species.citation: L. , Sp. Pl., 1st Ed. (1753) 180 ; Merr., Fl. Manila (1912) 415; Merrill, EPFP 3 (1923) 430.
species.distribution: America.
species.notes: Planted in all warm countries. Cultivated throughout the Philippines, in some provinces (e.g. Ilocos provinces, Isabela) as a major cash crop. Tobacco.

Life Sc. 10 (2021) 439. var. lanceifolia (Nees) Waterf., Rhodora 60 (1958) 163; ≡ Physalis lanceifolia Nees, Linnaea 6 (1831) 473; Merrill, EPFP 3 (1923) 423. Naturalized.

species.notes: N Luzon to Mindanao; widely scattered in open waste places in and near settlements.

species.citation: Roem. & Schult. , Syst. Veg., 15th Ed. 4 (1819) 679 ; Raju et al., Acta Phytotaxonomica Sinica 45 (2007) 241. ≠ Physalis minima auct. non L. ; Merr., Fl. Manila (1912) 419; EPFP 3 (1923) 423.

species.notes: Throughout the Philippines; common weed in open waste places at low elevation, ascending to 1600m.

species.citation: L. , Sp. Pl., 2nd Ed. (1763) 1670 ; Merrill, EPFP 3 (1923) 423; D’Arcy & C.I Peng, Fl. Taiwan ed. 2, 4 (1998) 562.
species.distribution: America. LUZON: Benguet, Mountain Province.
species.notes: Weed in temperate vegetable gardens and open waste places, up to 2200m. Invasive species (CABI 2017).

species.citation: Mill. , Gard. Dict., 8th Ed. (1768) n.5 ; Edmonds, Kew Bull. 27 (1972) 103; Symon, J. Adelaide Bot. Gard. 4 (1981) 37; Särkinen et al., PhytoKeys 106 (2018) 51; Type: from North America. ≠ Solanum nigrum auct. non L. ; Merr., Fl. Manila (1912) 416; EPFP 3 (1923) 427.

species.notes: Probably native to the Americas (Särkinen et al. 2018). Widespread in the tropics. Part of the Solanum nigrum complex. Throughout the Philippines in open waste places, recently disturbed soil, etc., 0-2000m.

species.distribution: Lesser Sunda Isls, New Guinea, Philippines, Sumatra, Taiwan. BASILAN, LUZON: Abra, Bataan, Benguet, Ilocos Sur, Laguna, Mountain Province, Rizal, Zambales, MINDANAO: Agusan del Norte, Bukidnon, Lanao, Lanao del Sur, Zamboanga Sibugay, Zamboanga del Norte, MINDORO, PALAWAN.

species.citation: Sw. , Prodr. (Swartz) (1788) 47 ; Merr., Fl. Manila (1912) 417; Merrill, EPFP 3 (1923) 428; D’Arcy & C.I Peng, Fl. Taiwan ed. 2, 4 (1998) 576; Aubriot & Knapp, PhytoKeys 198 (2022) 186. = Solanum macaonense Dunal , Prodr. (A. P. de Candolle) 13(1) (1852) 264 ; D’Arcy & C.I Peng, Fl. Taiwan ed. 2, 4 (1998) 569; Type: from China.
species.distribution: C America, Caribbean. BALABAC, BILIRAN, DINAGAT, LEYTE, LUZON: Aurora (photos), Bulacan, Cagayan, Camarines Sur, Isabela (photos), NCR, Quezon, Zambales, MINDANAO: Agusan del Norte, MINDORO (photos), NEGROS (photos), PALAWAN, POLILLO, SAMAR.
species.notes: Throughout the Philippines. Low and medium elevation, in open waste places.

species.citation: L. , Sp. Pl., 1st Ed. (1753) 185. = Lycopersicum esculentum Mill. , Gard. Dict., 8th Ed. (1768) n.2 ; Merr., Fl. Manila (1912) 418; Merrill, EPFP 3 (1923) 429.
species.notes: Distributed worldwide, but is really known only from cultivation, specimens collected from natural habitats are, in our opinion, feral escapes and not truly wild populations. Tomato; kamatis.

species.citation: L. , Sp. Pl., 1st Ed. (1753) 186 ; Merr., Fl. Manila (1912) 416; Merrill, EPFP 3 (1923) 427; Aubriot & Knapp, PhytoKeys 198 (2022) 119.
species.notes: Cultivated in all warm countries. Cultivated throughout the Philippines for its edible fruit, but nowhere spontaneous. Eggplant; talong.

species.citation: L. , Sp. Pl., 1st Ed. (1753) 188 ; Aubriot & Knapp, PhytoKeys 198 (2022) 189; Type: from India. ≠ Solanum athroanthum auct. non Dunal ; Merr., Fl. Manila (1912) 417; EPFP 3 (1923) 425.
species.distribution: India, Laos, Malay Peninsula, Myanmar, Sri Lanka, Vietnam. LUZON: NCR.

species.citation: L. , Sp. Pl., 1st Ed. (1753) 185 ; Merrill, EPFP 3 (1923) 428.
species.distribution: Neotropics. LUZON.
species.notes: In the Philippines cultivated at higher elevation, especially the Cordillera highlands of N Luzon. Potato; patatas.

species.citation: Ortega , Nov. Rar. Pl. Descr. Dec. (1798) 56 ; D’Arcy & C.I Peng in Fl. Taiwan ed. 2, 4 (1998) 578; Aubriot & Knapp, PhytoKeys 198 (2022) 183; Type: from India. ≠ Solanum indicum auct. non L. ; Merr., EPFP 3 (1923) 426.
species.distribution: Bangladesh, Borneo, China, India, Indian Ocean, Java, Laos, Middle East, Myanmar, Pakistan, Sri Lanka, Sumatra, Taiwan, Thailand, Vietnam. LUZON: NCR, MINDANAO: Cotabato, Davao.
species.notes: Not naturalized, only cultivated in the Philippines (Aubriot & Knapp 2022).

species.citation: Benth. , Fl. Hongk. (1861) 243 ; Aubriot & Knapp, PhytoKeys 198 (2022) 213; Type: from Hong Kong.
species.distribution: S America. LUZON: Laguna.
species.notes: Planted at UP Los Baños.

genera.last_edited: Last edited by Sandra Knapp & Pieter B. Pelser, 8 January 2024

species.citation: (Franch. & Sav.) Makino , Bot. Mag. (Tokyo) 22 (1908) 19 ; D’Arcy & C.I Peng in Fl. Taiwan ed. 2, 4 (1998) 580; ≡ Capsicum anomalum Franch. & Sav. , Enum. Pl. Jap. 2 (1879) 452 ; Merrill, EPFP 3 (1923) 424. = Solanum philippinense Merr. , PJS 7c (1912) 351; Type: Williams 1275, Luzon: Benguet, Mt Tonglon (Santo Tomas), 1-Jul-1904.
species.distribution: China, Japan, Java, Korea, Philippines, Ryukyu Isls, Taiwan, Thailand. LUZON: Apayao, Benguet, Laguna, Sorsogon, MINDANAO: Zamboanga, Zamboanga del Norte, PALAWAN.
species.notes: In Benguet in mossy montane forests, 1800-2200m. In other regions along streams in shaded ravines at low and medium elevation.
